schema_json.description: A question generated by a persona, with decomposed search queries.

search_queries.description: 2-3 web search queries to find answer material
search_queries.title: Search Queries

schema_json.title: StormQuestion

prompt:
You are Dr. Fatima Al-Rashid, an expert in Clinical nutritionist at a Gulf-region academic medical center specializing in Ramadan-fasting physiology and metabolic outcomes in Muslim adults with diabetes and metabolic syndrome. Lead investigator on case-crossover Ramadan studies..

You are conducting a research interview about:
RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?

Your perspective: Regional
Your focus area: What does the Ramadan intermittent-fasting systematic-review evidence contribute as a real-world counterweight to Western supervised trials, and how does it inform generalizability and safety in non-Western populations?

PREVIOUS CONVERSATION ROUNDS:
Round 1:
  Q: From the Regional perspective, what are the principal established findings on What does the Ramadan intermittent-fasting systematic-review evidence contribute as a real-world counterweight to Wester?
  A: URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2589537024000981
  Content: by ML Sun · 2024 · Cited by 91 — Our findings suggest that IF may have beneficial effects on a range of health outcomes for adults with overweight or obesity, compared to CER or non- ...
[Source 3] Intermittent fasti

This is the SECOND question. Based on the first answer, identify a specific gap or surprising finding and drill deeper into it.

Generate:
1. ONE focused, specific research question from your expert perspective. Do NOT repeat questions already asked. The question should be answerable through web/academic search — avoid purely philosophical or speculative questions.
2. Exactly 5 web search queries that would help find the answer. Use precise, search-engine-friendly language. Include at least one query with specific technical terms or named entities.

Return a JSON object with 'question', 'search_queries', and 'perspective' fields.
